source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $\angle B=46^\circ$, $\angle C=52^\circ$, $AD$ bisects $\angle BAC$, intersects $BC$ at point $D$, $DE \parallel AB$, and intersects $AC$ at point $E$, then what is the measure of $\angle ADE$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Given that $\angle B=46^\circ$ and $\angle C=52^\circ$,\\
thus $\angle BAC=180^\circ-\angle B-\angle C=180^\circ-46^\circ-52^\circ=82^\circ$,\\
and since AD bisects $\angle BAC$,\\
thus $\angle BAD=\frac{1}{2}\times82^\circ=41^\circ$,\\
since DE$\parallel$AB,\\
thus $\angle ADE=\boxed{41^\circ}$.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'As shown in the figure'
the problem uses geometric incidence/parallel relations tied to a diagram (AD bisects angle BAC, DE ∥ AB)
however, the key relations and labels are also fully stated in text, so the image may be helpful rather than strictly necessary
multi_step: solution requires combining multiple facts: compute angle BAC from angles B and C, then use angle bisector AD, then use DE ∥ AB to transfer the angle
this is more than a single direct lookup and involves at least 3 linked reasoning steps
the problem is still fairly short and standard, so the multi-step depth is moderate rather than very high
verifiability: the target answer is a single numeric angle measure (41°), which is easy to check
the provided answer_text gives an explicit derivation that can be step-verified
asset_registry_record shows registry_passed=true, question_present=true, and answer_present=true
